title: Pungent American Broccoli Stir-fry
description: Malty, snappy, and indulgent American dish featuring Chinese broccoli, tagine-cooked for a low-sodium-friendly twist. Perfect for a weeknight dinner in a traditional dolsot-stone-bowl.

ingredients.name: Vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: Use a low-smoke point oil like grapeseed or canola.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince or crush to release flavor.

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: Grate or finely chop to add depth.

ingredients.name: Soy sauce (low-sodium)
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste for sodium levels.

ingredients.name: Salt (optional)
ingredients.notes: Low-sodium dish, use sparingly if necessary.

ingredients.name: Chopped fresh cilantro
ingredients.notes: A pop of color and freshness.

instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the Chinese broccoli and cook, stirring occasionally, for 4-5 minutes or until it starts to soften.
Add the soy sauce, maple syrup, black pepper, garam masala, and cayenne pepper (if using) and stir to combine.
Continue cooking, stirring occasionally, for another 2-3 minutes or until the broccoli is tender but still crisp.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Transfer the stir-fry to a dolsot-stone-bowl or individual serving dishes.
Serve immediately, garnished with chopped green onions and a side of cooked brown rice and chopped fresh cilantro.
Garnish with additional cilantro if desired.
